title: Vitis davidi
taxonomy: Vitis davidi is classified as a species of grapevine in the genus Vitis. It belongs to the family Vitaceae, commonly known as the grape family. The species Vitis davidi is native to China. It is closely related to other grapevine species such as Vitis amurensis and Vitis vinifera. The exact classification of Vitis davidi is still being studied and verified by botanists.
uses: Vitis davidi, also known as the Golden Sapling or Chinese wild grape, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its purported health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts are also thought to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for infections and skin problems. Furthermore, Vitis davidi is used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

properties: Vitis davidi, also known as the golden grape, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's extracts have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which may help in the treatment of various infections.
